scope: DART exact-FBF four-cell outcome traces plus a timed mu=0.5, omega=2 visual capture

source_claim.keyframe_note: The source frames were inspected from a temporary local copy and are not repository goldens or redistributed deliverables.

capture.command: out=docs/dev_tasks/fbf_exact_coulomb_friction/assets/paper_evidence/fig04_turntable; args=(--scene fbf_paper_turntable --headless --steps 240 --width 1280 --height 720 --threads 1 --headless-sidecar $out/timeline.json); for step in 0 60 120 180 240; do args+=(--headless-shot-at $step:$out/frames/frame_$(printf '%03d' $step).png); done; build/default/cpp/Release/bin/dart-demos ${args[@]}
capture.note: The existing demo exposes only the captured paper cell non-interactively; the four-cell outcome is therefore supplied by separate deterministic trace rows, not falsely presented as a four-cell visual.

visual_claims.claim: The locally reconstructed mu=0.5, omega=2 rider remains captured and co-rotates through T=4 s.
visual_claims.support: The blue rider remains on the table in all five inspected frames, follows an orbit in the compact clip, and finishes at radius 1.11715 m with 4 contacts in the tracked trace.

visual_claims.claim: The four DART trace outcomes match the paper's qualitative ejected/ejected/captured/ejected order.
visual_claims.support: At T=4 s only mu=0.5, omega=2 retains 4 contacts at z=0.1239 m; the other three rows have zero contacts, radii 6.35-23.57 m, and negative heights after leaving the support.

visual_claims.claim: Every shown or traced DART row used exact FBF without boxed-LCP fallback.
visual_claims.support: The visual sidecar and all four trajectory CSVs report zero fallbacks and residual no larger than 9.99915e-7.

composition.panel_preparation_command: for step in 0 60 120 180 240; do .pixi/envs/gazebo/bin/ffmpeg -y -i frames/frame_$(printf '%03d' $step).png -vf crop=660:506:260:58 -frames:v 1 panels/frame_$(printf '%03d' $step).png; done

video.note: This compact DART-only clip covers only the captured cell, not the paper video's simultaneous four-cell 15-second edit.

image_verification.strict_contrast_note: The optional generic contrast gate rejects the dark turntable frames and cropped sheet; the failures are retained. The rider, support, time labels, and exact-solver diagnostics remain legible in the inspected outputs.

unmet_requirements: The local visual uses a square support while the paper uses a circular segmented disk; it is a qualitative reconstruction, not source geometry parity.
The existing non-interactive demo renders only mu=0.5, omega=2; a source-ordered four-cell visual matrix is still missing.
The paper does not publish its ramp duration or snapshot time; the local protocol uses a documented one-second linear ramp and T=4 s.
No approved source golden or golden diff exists.
The compact DART clip is 4.07 seconds and lacks the paper video's simultaneous four-cell 15-second edit.
